title: hypericum brachyphyllum
intro: Hypericum brachyphyllum, also known as St. John's Wort Dwarf, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of hypericin and hyperforin. The plant has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, depression, and insomnia, as well as to soothe wounds and promote wound healing. Hypericum brachyphyllum can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
